articles.headline: TSMC rides the AI boom as first-quarter sales jump 35%
articles.summary: TSMC reported Q1 revenue up 35% year-over-year to $35.7 billion, exceeding forecasts due to surging demand for AI chips. This signals sustained heavy spending by hyperscalers on AI infrastructure, pressuring supply chains for packaging, memory, power, and cooling. Businesses reliant on advanced semiconductors face ongoing capacity constraints and rising costs.

articles.headline: Motorola raises budget phone prices up to 50% due to AI and memory shortages
articles.summary: Motorola hiked prices on budget phones by up to 50%, blaming AI-driven chip and memory shortages that disrupt consumer device supply chains. This exacerbates affordability issues for budget hardware, signaling broader impacts on device makers and businesses evaluating mobile tech upgrades. SMEs and local businesses face higher costs for digital tools amid persistent semiconductor constraints.

articles.headline: Intel Joins Elon Musk’s Terafab Chip Project for Tesla and xAI
articles.summary: Intel partnered with Elon Musk’s Terafab initiative to develop semiconductors for Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, boosting Intel shares by 4.2% and signaling shifts in AI chip supply chains. This could diversify sourcing away from Asian manufacturers, impacting global trade and hardware costs for AI infrastructure. Businesses gain potential new options amid ongoing chip shortages.
